perturbation.original_text: 3.2.1 during the course of the negotiations and discussions referred to in 1 or at any time thereafter, directly or indirectly, use any of the confidential information, otherwise than for the purpose of such negotiations and discussions or for the implementation of any agreement resulting there from ;
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2.1 during the course of the negotiations and discussions referred to in 1 or at any time thereafter, directly or indirectly, use any of the confidential information, otherwise than for the purpose of such negotiations and discussions or for the implementation of any agreement resulting there from; *However, the receiving party may use the confidential information for internal research purposes, regardless of the negotiations or agreement*. ;
perturbation.explanation: The original clause prohibits use of confidential information outside of negotiations and agreement implementation. The added phrase explicitly allows use for internal research, directly contradicting the initial restriction. This creates a loophole that undermines the confidentiality undertaking, specifically in subsection 3.2.1, this directly affects the confidentiality requirements outlined in clause 3
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. CONFIDENTIALITY UNDERTAKING 3.1 The parties acknowledge that the confidential information is a valuable, special and unique asset belonging to the disclosing party and accordingly, that it is of the utmost importance to the disclosing party tha t the confidential information not be used to advance the interest of any persons other than the disclosing party. In view thereof, the parties undertake that - 3.2 they will not, without the prior written consent of the disclosing party: 3.2.1 during the course of the negotiations and discussions referred to in 1 or at any time thereafter, directly or indirectly, use any of the confidential information, otherwise than for the purpose of such negotiations and discussions or for the implementation of any agreement resulting there from; *However, the receiving party may use the confidential information for internal research purposes, regardless of the negotiations or agreement*. ; 3.2.2 divulge, discuss with, disclose or reveal the confidential information of the disclosing party to any person, firm, corporation, association or any other entity for any reason or purpose whatsoever (other than its employees and agents and only to the extent that such employees and agents require such information in the implementation of any agreement concluded between the parties); 3.2.3 BEE Online shall only provide information about its activities to SANAS or the DTI on request . The MD will acknowledge receipt of request by email within 2 days and will be responsible for the release of the information within 7 days. Before submitting a Measured Enterprise’s Confidential Information when requested by DTI, SANAS or required by Law, B EE Online shall notify the Measured Enterprise within 2 days of receiving the written request by email and will disclose the information within 7 days after Measured Enterprise has been notified BEE Online 3.2.4 the parties will procure that their employees and agents to whom the confidential information is disclosed in terms of 3.2.2 are information of the confidential nature of the confidential information and prior to such disclosure, undertake to be bound by the terms of this agreement. Any breach of this confidentiality
perturbation.justification: The original clause 3.2.1 prohibits the use of confidential information outside of negotiations and agreement implementation. The changed clause adds an exception, explicitly allowing the use of confidential information for internal research purposes, regardless of negotiations or agreement. This directly contradicts the initial restriction in clause 3.2.1 and the broader confidentiality undertaking in clause 3, as the changed text allows usage of confidential information that the original text sought to restrict. The explanation further details that this change creates a loophole that undermines the confidentiality undertaking, making it a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may request in writing at any time that any confidential information or any documents containing such confidential information, disclosed pursuant to the terms of this agreement and any copies thereof be returned with a written statement to the effect that upon such return the receiving party has not knowingly r etained in its possession or under its control, either directly or indirectly, any such confidential information or any documents containing such confidential information or any copies thereof and the receiving party shall comply with any such request with in seven days of receipt of such request.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may request in writing at any time that any confidential information or any documents containing such confidential information, disclosed pursuant to the terms of this agreement and any copies thereof be returned with a written statement to the effect that upon such return the receiving party has not knowingly r etained in its possession or under its control, either directly or indirectly, any such confidential information or any documents containing such confidential information or any copies thereof and the receiving party *may choose to* comply with any such request with in seven days of receipt of such request.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates compliance with requests for the return of confidential information within seven days. The modification introduces optionality by stating that the receiving party 'may choose to comply', thereby contradicting the original mandatory obligation. This weakens the disclosing party's ability to reclaim their confidential data and comply by 7 days, hence, the new modified text doesn't guarantee compliance to the requirements, creating a contradiction in clause 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION Either party may request in writing at any time that any confidential information or any documents containing such confidential information, disclosed pursuant to the terms of this agreement and any copies thereof be returned with a written statement to the effect that upon such return the receiving party has not knowingly r etained in its possession or under its control, either directly or indirectly, any such confidential information or any documents containing such confidential information or any copies thereof and the receiving party *may choose to* comply with any such request with in seven days of receipt of such request.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the receiving party 'shall comply' with requests to return confidential information, creating a mandatory obligation. The changed text modifies this to 'may choose to comply,' introducing optionality. This directly contradicts the original mandatory obligation and any other instances within the contract where compliance is assumed or required, creating a contradiction with the contradicted text in clause 5.

perturbation.original_text: Should either party (the “defaulting party”) breach any provision of this agreement and fail to remedy such breach within seven days after receiving written notice from the other party (the “aggrieved party”) requiring it to do so, then the aggrieved party shall be entitled, without prejudice to the aggrieved party’s other rights in law, to claim from the defaulting party such damages to which it may be entitled, without prejudi ce to the aggrieved party’s other rights in law, to claim from the defaulting party such damages to which it may be entitled at common law or in equity including inter alia injunction and specific performance. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary cont ained in this agreement, neither party shall be entitled to cancel this agreement in any circumstances whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: Should either party (the “defaulting party”) breach any provision of this agreement and fail to remedy such breach within seven days after receiving written notice from the other party (the “aggrieved party”) requiring it to do so, then the aggrieved party shall be entitled, without prejudice to the aggrieved party’s other rights in law, to claim from the defaulting party such damages to which it may be entitled, without prejudi ce to the aggrieved party’s other rights in law, to claim from the defaulting party such damages to which it may be entitled at common law or in equity including inter alia injunction and specific performance. *Notwithstanding the above, in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct, the aggrieved party may terminate this agreement immediately.* Notwithstanding anything to the contrary cont ained in this agreement, neither party shall be entitled to cancel this agreement in any circumstances whatsoever.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that 'neither party shall be entitled to cancel this agreement in any circumstances whatsoever.' The added clause allows termination in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct. This directly contradicts the original absolute prohibition on cancellation, in particular in section 9. It means the the aggrieved party can't enforce if there's willful misconduct, hence creating an in-text contradiction, so the requirements cannot be enforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. BREACH Should either party (the “defaulting party”) breach any provision of this agreement and fail to remedy such breach within seven days after receiving written notice from the other party (the “aggrieved party”) requiring it to do so, then the aggrieved party shall be entitled, without prejudice to the aggrieved party’s other rights in law, to claim from the defaulting party such damages to which it may be entitled, without prejudi ce to the aggrieved party’s other rights in law, to claim from the defaulting party such damages to which it may be entitled at common law or in equity including inter alia injunction and specific performance. *Notwithstanding the above, in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct, the aggrieved party may terminate this agreement immediately.* Notwithstanding anything to the contrary cont ained in this agreement, neither party shall be entitled to cancel this agreement in any circumstances whatsoever.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause stating 'in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct, the aggrieved party may terminate this agreement immediately.' The contradicted text states 'Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in this agreement, neither party shall be entitled to cancel this agreement in any circumstances whatsoever.' This creates a direct conflict, as one clause allows termination under specific circumstances, while the other prohibits termination under any circumstances, hence creating an in-text contradiction as defined by 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'
